Short-answer; papers provided. The stem supplies all required comparative facts (4× gas per energy, 6.6× peak mass-loss-rate per energy, wider ELR, ~14× shorter valve-to-TR interval) and asks for a reasoning-based refutation of “energy-matched implies equal hazard.” The gold answer’s atomic claims follow directly from these given normalized differences via unit/scale reasoning (equal energy => absolute quantities scale with per-energy metrics; ELR is a range property independent of total gas; timing is independent of energy). No hidden paper-only definitions are required, and none of the claims contradict the evidence bundle.
